commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=74a68ccc8f5a3303a4760f1009c712bd536715d4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens `device.pair.approve` so every non-admin caller checks the pending request before approving non-operator device roles, with server-method regressions for shared-auth operator and node approvals.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +71. Total +71 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, with high confidence but without a live run. Current main sets `callerDeviceId` only for device-token auth and skips the pending role gate for shared-auth sessions, allowing the path to fall through to `approveDevicePairing`.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer handling for the protected gateway authz compatibility change and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation should not repair while the proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(gateway): require admin for device role approvals
target.body:
## Summary

Require non-admin `device.pair.approve` callers to pass the non-operator device-role gate regardless of whether the session was authenticated with a device token or shared auth.

## Changes

- Load the pending pairing request for every non-admin approval attempt.
- Keep the existing cross-device ownership check limited to sessions with a device-token-derived caller device id.
- Add regressions for shared-auth/trusted-proxy-shaped sessions: operator-role approvals still succeed, and node-role approvals are denied before `approveDevicePairing` runs.

## Validation

Behavior addressed: Non-admin pairing-only callers can no longer approve non-operator device roles through `device.pair.approve` unless they also have `operator.admin`.
Real environment tested: Local source checkout on branch `688`, Node `v22.22.2`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/devices.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/devices.ts src/gateway/server-methods/devices.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/server-methods/devices.ts src/gateway/server-methods/devices.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`.
Evidence after fix: The focused Vitest run passed with 2 files and 64 tests; formatting and file-scoped oxlint passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: The new shared-auth regression denies a non-admin node-role approval before calling `approveDevicePairing`, while the operator-role shared-auth regression remains allowed.
What was not tested: I did not run a live trusted-proxy WebSocket gateway scenario or the full repository check suite; CI should cover broader lanes.

## Notes

- AI-assisted by Codex.
- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
- `USER.md` was updated locally with the requested worklog and is intentionally not part of this PR.
target.author: eleqtrizit
